title: Multiplication Game
description:
Alice and Bob are in their class doing drills on multiplication and division. They quickly get bored and instead decide to play a game they invented.

The game starts with a target integer N ≥ 2, and an integer M = 1. Alice and Bob take alternate turns. At each turn, the player chooses a prime divisor p of N, and multiply M by p. If the player’s move makes the value of M equal to the target N, the player wins. If M > N, the game is a tie.

Assuming that both players play optimally, who (if any) is going to win?
input: The first line of input contains T (1 ≤ T ≤ 10000), the number of cases to follow. Each of the next T lines describe a case. Each case is specified by N (2 ≤ N ≤ 231 − 1) followed by the name of the player making the first turn. The name is either Alice or Bob.
output: For each case, print the name of the winner (Alice or Bob) assuming optimal play, or tie if there is no winner.

samples.input:
10
10 Alice
20 Bob
30 Alice
40 Bob
50 Alice
60 Bob
70 Alice
80 Bob
90 Alice
100 Bob
samples.output:
Bob
Bob
tie
tie
Alice
tie
tie
tie
tie
Alice

source: , ICPC, Regionals, North America, Rocky Mountain Regional, 2017 Rocky Mountain Regional Contest
